correction.rationale: Placing negative points in the falsely included background region along the instep slope and positive points on the foot body will guide SAM to snap the boundary back to the true skin edge.

judgments.evidence: The mask is too loose along the entire upper instep of the foot, including background pixels between the ankle and the big toe.

observations.contour: The contour line deviates from the skin boundary on the upper dorsal area of the foot, leaving a visible gap of background pixels inside the mask.
observations.full_context: The character's right foot is positioned in the foreground (viewer's right).
observations.mask: The binary mask has a clean shape but appears slightly bloated along the upper instep slope.
observations.neighbor_overlap: No neighbor overlap issues are present.
observations.overlay: The red overlay clearly reveals that the mask boundary is too loose along the top-right instep of the foot, extending into the gray background.
observations.source_crop: The high-resolution crop shows the right foot clearly against a gray background.

correction.rationale: Use positive points throughout the visible foot and toes, with negative points across the lower shin and upper ankle contamination. SAM refinement should be safer than a polygon because the toe separations and irregular ankle boundary require pixel-level tracing.

judgments.evidence: The mask's top edge is around normalized y=100 while the visible ankle-to-foot transition is substantially lower, so the mask includes lower-leg area. The distal contour also clips the lowest visible toe/sole pixels. Character side remains right per COCO semantic chain.

observations.contour: The contour follows the outer foot reasonably well along the instep and sides, but the top contour is anatomically misplaced and the bottom contour is slightly tight around the toes.
observations.full_context: The candidate is the character's right foot, consistent with the supplied semantic pose-chain evidence; the foot is the forward foot in the crossed-leg pose.
observations.mask: The mask extends too far upward into the lower leg with an artificial near-horizontal cutoff, rather than following the ankle-to-foot boundary. It also appears to trim a small amount from the lowest toe/sole contour.
observations.neighbor_overlap: The main contamination is contiguous lower-leg/ankle skin belonging to the neighboring right ankle/calf region; no clothing or background is included.
observations.overlay: Red coverage clearly includes the lower shin/ankle region above the foot and has a block-like top edge. The lower edge is slightly inside the visible toe/sole boundary.
observations.source_crop: The visible target is a foot extending from the ankle into the toes. The upper portion of the crop visibly includes lower-leg skin above the anatomical ankle transition.

correction.rationale: Mask boundary and coverage are accurate and match the semantic right_foot_base target; no correction needed.

judgments.evidence: Mask corresponds to the correct side (right foot per COCO pose-chain) and provides accurate, complete coverage of the visible foot without contamination.

observations.contour: White contour line hugs the foot boundary accurately along top of foot, heel, and toes, with no gaps or excess extension into background.
observations.full_context: Cyan ROI box correctly isolates the forward-stepping foot; pose-chain evidence confirms this limb belongs to the right leg (ankle joint distance 263px vs 788px for left), matching the right_foot_base target.
observations.mask: Binary mask cleanly covers the foot silhouette from ankle to toe tips, following the foot's natural contour including the ankle notch at top.
observations.neighbor_overlap: No overlap with the other leg/foot or background gray floor is visible; boundary stays within the foot's visible pixels.
observations.overlay: Red overlay aligns tightly with visible foot skin; toes are fully included, ankle cut-off is reasonable and does not bleed into calf.
observations.source_crop: High-res crop shows a full lateral view of a bare foot with toes, dorsum, and ankle transition, no clothing or footwear present.
